label: PDF to JPEG
description: Converts PDF pages to JPEG images using Ghostscript via AWS Lambda. Each page becomes a separate file entity linked to the source PDF.
detailed_description:
## Overview

The PDF to JPEG klados converts PDF documents into individual JPEG images, one per page. It uses a Lambda backend with Ghostscript for high-quality rendering and supports configurable DPI, quality, and maximum dimensions.

## How It Works

1. Receives a target entity with a PDF file attachment
2. Starts an async job on the Lambda backend, passing entity ID and options
3. Lambda downloads the PDF from Arke, renders each page to JPEG, uploads results
4. Worker polls Lambda for completion using Durable Object alarms
5. On success, returns the IDs of created JPEG entities for workflow handoff

## When to Use

- Converting scanned PDFs for OCR processing
- Extracting images from digital PDFs for analysis
- Creating page thumbnails or previews
- Part of document processing pipelines (PDF → JPEG → OCR → text extraction)

## Limitations

- Requires Lambda backend with Ghostscript (not standalone)
- Large PDFs may take several minutes to process
- Very high DPI settings increase processing time and storage
- Password-protected PDFs are not supported

entity.types_description: Any entity type is accepted as long as it has a PDF file attachment. Common types include 'document', 'file', or domain-specific types.

label.description: Name of the source document. Used in generated page entity labels (e.g., 'Document.pdf - Page 1').

properties.notes: Properties are read from the target entity but not required. The key requirement is a PDF file attachment.

file.description: PDF file attachment on the target entity. If multiple files are attached, use target_file_key in klados_input to specify which one.

klados_input.description: Parameters passed via workflow input or request.input to customize PDF rendering.

target_file_key.description: Specific file key to process when entity has multiple file attachments. If not specified, uses the first PDF found.

options.description: PDF rendering options passed to Ghostscript

quality.description: JPEG quality from 1-100. Higher values produce larger files with better quality.

dpi.description: Render resolution in dots per inch. 300 DPI is good for OCR, 150 DPI for previews.

max_dimension.description: Maximum width or height in pixels. Images exceeding this are resized proportionally.

creates.types_description: Each PDF page becomes a 'file' type entity with a JPEG attachment.

page_number.description: 1-indexed page number from the source PDF

source_entity_id.description: ID of the source PDF entity

label.condition: When source entity has a label

page_of.description: Links the page image back to its source PDF

file_attachment.description: JPEG image of the rendered PDF page

modifies.description: The source PDF entity is updated with relationships to all created page entities.

has_page.description: Links source PDF to each of its page images

handoff.outputs: IDs of all created JPEG page entities
handoff.description: Passes entity IDs of all page images to the next workflow step. Pages are ordered by page_number. Typically feeds into OCR processing.

processing.tier_description: Uses Durable Objects with SQL state for Lambda polling. The DO schedules alarms to check Lambda job status until completion.
processing.typical_duration: 10-60 seconds for typical documents (5-20 pages). Large documents (100+ pages) may take several minutes.
processing.idempotent_description: Re-running on the same PDF will create duplicate page entities. Downstream deduplication is recommended if duplicates are a concern.

pdf_size.notes: Larger PDFs are supported but increase Lambda processing time and may approach Lambda timeout limits.

page_count.notes: Higher page counts work but increase total processing time.

constraints.lambda_timeout: Lambda has a 15-minute timeout. Very large PDFs may fail if rendering exceeds this.
constraints.concurrency: Safe to run multiple instances in parallel. Each job gets its own DO keyed by job_id, and Lambda handles concurrent jobs independently.

no_pdf_attachment.message: No PDF file found on entity

lambda_start_failure.behavior: Throws error with HTTP status and message

lambda_processing_error.behavior: Throws error after Lambda reports failure

corrupt_pdf.behavior: Lambda returns error during rendering phase

side_effects: Creates file entities in target_collection (one per page)
Uploads JPEG files to Arke storage via Lambda
Updates source PDF entity with has_page relationships
Consumes Lambda compute time and network bandwidth

examples.name: Basic PDF conversion
examples.description: Convert a simple PDF document to JPEG pages with default settings

examples.name: Custom quality for OCR
examples.description: Convert PDF with high DPI settings optimized for OCR accuracy
examples.notes: Higher DPI and quality settings produce larger images that are better for OCR but take longer to process.

examples.name: Thumbnail generation
examples.description: Convert PDF with lower quality settings for quick previews
examples.notes: Lower settings produce smaller files faster, suitable for thumbnails or previews.

often_used_with.label: OCR Processor
often_used_with.description: Extracts text from JPEG images using vision models.
often_used_with.when: After PDF to JPEG conversion, to extract text from scanned documents.

often_used_with.label: Text Chunker
often_used_with.description: Splits extracted text into manageable chunks.

often_used_with.label: Page Assembler
often_used_with.description: Combines OCR results from multiple pages into a single text.
often_used_with.when: After OCR processing all pages, to reconstruct the full document text.

workflows.label: pdf-processing
workflows.description: Full pipeline: PDF → JPEG pages → OCR → assemble → chunk → extract

workflows.label: file-processing
workflows.description: Unified entry point that routes files by type to appropriate pipelines

costs.lambda_invocations: 1 Lambda invocation per PDF (polling is HTTP, not Lambda)
costs.compute_time: Lambda compute scales with page count and DPI settings
costs.storage: JPEG files stored in Arke, size depends on quality/DPI settings
costs.estimated_cost: $0.01-0.10 per document depending on page count and settings
